steps.title: Read the graphed line
steps.content: The graph of $y=f(x)+13$ passes through $(-3,8)$ and $(1,4)$. Slope $=\dfrac{4-8}{1-(-3)}=\dfrac{-4}{4}=-1$, so the line is $y=-x+5$.

steps.title: Match the form with positive c, d
Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $-d-cx$ — negative slope ($-c$), negative y-intercept ($-d$). Matches $f(x)=-x-8$.
  • B) $d-cx$ — negative slope but positive y-intercept; f has y-intercept $-8<0$. Eliminate.
  • C) $d+cx$ — positive slope; f has slope $-1<0$. Eliminate.
  • D) $-d+cx$ — positive slope; eliminate.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
